UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information and application settings within CozyJet.AI. This entity stores core user data and customizable application preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's unique email address, used for authentication and communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

settings.description: A JSON string containing various user-specific application settings, including agent toggles, privacy preferences, UI appearance, and Skippy's configuration. This allows for flexible storage of diverse settings. Example: { "agents": { "skippyEnabled": true, "flippoEnabled": true, "snooksEnabled": false }, "skippyConfig": { "deepWorkSessionLength": 30, "sensitivityThreshold": 0.7 }, "appearance": { "darkMode": true }, "privacy": { "localOnlyMode": false } }

SkippyEncryptedData.title: SkippyEncryptedData
SkippyEncryptedData.description: Stores an AES-256 encrypted binary blob of Skippy's local screen analysis data. The server persists this opaque data unit without decryption, ensuring user privacy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SkippyEncryptedData entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this encrypted data belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SkippyEncryptedData)

dataBlob.description: The encrypted binary data blob from Skippy's local processing, typically stored as a base64 encoded string.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this encrypted data blob was first created or saved.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this encrypted data blob was last updated.

ProductivitySession.title: ProductivitySession
ProductivitySession.description: Represents a summary of a user's work session, generated by the Flippo AI agent, including overall productivity metrics and a narrative summary.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductivitySession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this productivity session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ProductivitySession)

startTime.description: The start timestamp of the productivity session.

endTime.description: The end timestamp of the productivity session.

totalDurationMinutes.description: The total duration of the session in minutes.

deepWorkScore.description: Flippo's calculated deep work score for this session, typically a value out of 100.

title.description: An auto-generated, concise title summarizing the session, e.g., 'Morning client proposal work'.

summary.description: A detailed narrative summary of the session generated by Flippo.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this productivity session record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this productivity session record was last updated.

TimelineEntry.title: TimelineEntry
TimelineEntry.description: Represents a specific activity or event within a ProductivitySession, detailing focused work blocks or distraction spikes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TimelineEntry entity.

sessionId.description: Reference to the ProductivitySession this entry belongs to. (Relationship: ProductivitySession 1:N TimelineEntry)

description.description: A concise one-line description of the activity, e.g., 'Wrote client proposal draft' or 'Checked social media'.

activityType.description: The type of activity, indicating focus level (e.g., 'deep work', 'shallow work', 'distraction').

durationMinutes.description: The duration of this specific activity in minutes.

timestamp.description: The start timestamp of this specific activity.

screenshotReference.description: An optional client-side reference to a blurred screenshot thumbnail that is stored locally within Skippy's encrypted data. This is not a direct server-stored image URL.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this timeline entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this timeline entry was last updated.

SnooksConversation.title: SnooksConversation
SnooksConversation.description: Represents a distinct conversation thread with the Snooks AI agent for generating marketing content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SnooksConversation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SnooksConversation)

title.description: A short, descriptive title for the conversation, often auto-generated based on the initial prompt.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was initiated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation was last updated or a message was added.

SnooksMessage.title: SnooksMessage
SnooksMessage.description: Represents an individual message exchanged within a SnooksConversation, encompassing both user prompts and AI-generated responses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SnooksMessage entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the SnooksConversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: SnooksConversation 1:N SnooksMessage)

sender.description: Indicates whether the message was sent by 'User' or 'Snooks'.

content.description: The textual content of the message.

isUserMessage.description: A boolean flag indicating if the message was sent by the user (true) or Snooks (false).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent or received.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the message was last updated (e.g., edited by user).

GeneratedContentOutput.title: GeneratedContentOutput
GeneratedContentOutput.description: Stores the specific marketing content generated by the Snooks AI agent for a particular platform, along with associated strategy and image references.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedContentOutput entity.

messageId.description: Reference to the SnooksMessage that initiated the generation of this content. (Relationship: SnooksMessage 1:N GeneratedContentOutput)

platform.description: The target platform for the generated content (e.g., 'LinkedIn', 'X', 'Instagram', 'Gmail', 'YouTube Script').

content.description: The full text content generated by Snooks for the specified platform.

imagePrompt.description: Optional: The prompt used for generating an accompanying image (if applicable).

imageUrl.description: Optional: A URL to the generated image, if any, hosted externally.

viralStrategy.description: A JSON string containing the suggested viral strategy for this content, including posting schedule, hashtags, optimal times, and engagement hooks. This allows for flexible schema changes without database migrations. Example: { "postingSchedule": "Monday, Wednesday, Friday at 10 AM EST", "hashtagStrategy": "Mix of broad and niche #hashtags", "optimalPostingTime": "Mornings for B2B, evenings for B2C", "engagementHooks": "Ask questions, create polls" }

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this content output was generated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this content output was last updated (e.g., after editing by the user).

WaitlistSubscriber.title: WaitlistSubscriber
WaitlistSubscriber.description: Represents an individual who has subscribed to the CozyJet.AI waitlist for future product announcements.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WaitlistSubscriber entity.

email.description: The email address of the waitlist subscriber.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscriber joined the waitlist.

definition.description: Stores core user profile information and application settings. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId}.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Stores AES-256 encrypted binary blobs of Skippy's local screen analysis data. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId}. The document includes 'userId' for robust authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

params.name: skippyEncryptedDataId
params.description: The unique ID of a specific Skippy encrypted data blob.

definition.description: Stores summaries of user work sessions generated by Flippo. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId}. The document includes 'userId' for robust authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique ID of a specific productivity session.

definition.description: Stores individual activity entries within a ProductivitySession. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId}. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, crucial for secure list operations without hierarchical dependencies.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent productivity session.

params.name: entryId
params.description: The unique ID of a specific timeline entry.

params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of a specific Snooks conversation.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a SnooksConversation. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId}. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, crucial for secure list operations without hierarchical dependencies.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent Snooks conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of a specific Snooks message.

definition.description: Stores marketing content generated by Snooks for a specific message. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId}. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, crucial for secure list operations without hierarchical dependencies.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication's UID.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent Snooks conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent Snooks message.

params.name: outputId
params.description: The unique ID of a specific generated content output.

definition.description: Stores email addresses of users who have joined the waitlist. Allows public creation but restricts read/update/delete to administrative backend processes.

params.name: subscriberId
params.description: The unique ID of a waitlist subscriber.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Query as Authorization Policy (QAP)** by employing a deeply hierarchical, user-centric pathing strategy combined with essential denormalization. All user-specific data is rooted under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This ensures that a user's entire data footprint is securely segmented and directly tied to their authenticated UID (`request.auth.uid`).

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
For entities nested deeper than the direct user profile (e.g., `TimelineEntry`, `SnooksMessage`, `GeneratedContentOutput`), their security posture depends on the owning `userId` of their ancestral `ProductivitySession` or `SnooksConversation`. To eliminate costly and insecure `get()` calls in Firestore security rules, the `userId` of the owner is denormalized and stored directly within each document of these subcollections. For example, a `TimelineEntry` document, though nested under `/users/{userId}/productivitySessions/{sessionId}/timelineEntries/{entryId}`, will explicitly contain a `userId` field. This allows security rules to simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for any read or write operation on that document, ensuring atomic operations and highly efficient rule evaluation without needing to traverse up the hierarchy or fetch parent documents.

**Support for Query as Authorization Policy (QAP):**
By structuring all user-specific data under `/users/{userId}/...`, `list` operations are inherently secure. When a user queries for their `ProductivitySessions` (e.g., `/users/{current_uid}/productivitySessions`), the path itself enforces that only data belonging to `current_uid` can be retrieved. The denormalized `userId` within nested documents further strengthens this: even if a malicious query attempted to list data from an incorrect parent path, the document-level `userId` check would prevent unauthorized access. For example, a rule for `timelineEntries` would be `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId && request.auth.uid == userId;`, where `userId` is the wildcard from the path. This makes it impossible to query for another user's data without explicit permission, as the `userId` in the path must match both the authenticated user and the `userId` denormalized within the document.

**Structural Segregation and Access Modeling:**
All collections maintain a homogeneous security posture. `/users/{userId}` serves as the root for all private user data. The `/waitlistSubscribers` collection is a top-level collection due to its different access requirements (public write for sign-up, restricted read for administration). No `members` maps are needed for these entities as all data is strictly private to a single user.

This design ensures simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules, adhering to all core design principles and strategic mandates.
